meta.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive — Member State Tracker
meta.subtitle: Where the 27 Member States Stand
meta.disclaimer: Status and sources verified for ISO week 2026-W20 (10 May 2026). This page does not constitute legal advice.

green.label: Transposed or on-track
green.definition: National law adopted, or final bill confirmed to enter into force on or before 7 June 2026.

amber.label: Draft published
amber.definition: Bill or decree in formal consultation or before parliament; intent clear, deadline at risk.

red.label: No public draft / resisting
red.definition: No formal text published, only initial steps announced — or government has signalled non-compliance.

directivePillars: Pay range disclosed in job ads / before interview
Right of employees to information on pay & criteria
Ban on salary-history questions of candidates
Joint pay assessment if unjustified gap > 5%
Reversed burden of proof in equal-pay litigation

directiveSources.title: European Commission confirms 7 June 2026 deadline holds

Bulgaria: 29 May target slipping — no draft tabled in National Assembly
Italy: definitive Council of Ministers approval on 30 April — second EU country to fully transpose in time

SK.name: Slovakia
SK.headline: Equal Pay Act adopted 15 Apr 2026; effective 7 Jun 2026 — first MS
SK.summary: NR SR adopted Zákon o rovnakom odmeňovaní by constitutional majority on 15 Apr 2026; effective 7 Jun 2026. Pay-structure deadline 31 Jul 2026; first reports (250+) by 7 Jun 2027. Includes same-sex equal-pay comparator (gold-plating beyond Directive).

keyDates.label: First pay-gap report (≥150 staff, covers 1 Aug–31 Dec 2026)

sources.title: NR SR schválila zákon o rovnakej odmene
Equal Pay Act passed to implement Gender Pay Transparency Directive
First past the post: Slovakia becomes first EU MS to transpose

SK.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: still the first MS across the line; gold-plated same-sex comparator confirmed.

IT.name: Italy
IT.headline: CdM final approval of legislative decree on 30 April 2026
IT.summary: Council of Ministers gave definitive (esame definitivo) approval to the legislative decree transposing Directive 2023/970 on 30 Apr 2026, after preliminary approval on 5 Feb. Entry into force expected by 7 Jun 2026. Awaiting publication in Gazzetta Ufficiale.

keyDates.label: Preliminary approval by Council of Ministers

sources.title: Approvato in via definitiva il Decreto legislativo sulla trasparenza retributiva
Parità e trasparenza retributiva: nuovi obblighi e sanzioni
Trasparenza retributiva, primo sì del Cdm

IT.weeklyCheckSummary: Strengthened: definitive CdM approval on 30 Apr — Italy on track to be the second EU MS to fully transpose in time.

MT.name: Malta
MT.headline: Partial transposition in force since 27 Aug 2025; full draft pending
MT.summary: Legal Notice amending the Employment and Industrial Relations Act in force since 27 Aug 2025 (pay-range disclosure, salary-history ban). Full transposition draft still not published; MEA has requested postponement. Partial-only transposition; remaining pay-equity provisions still outstanding.

sources.title: Malta implements EU Pay Transparency Directive

MT.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: confirms partial-only transposition; MEA pushing for postponement of remaining pay-equity provisions.

FI.name: Finland
FI.headline: Bill delayed: Eduskunta to receive proposal at midsummer; force late autumn 2026
FI.summary: STM Minister Grahn-Laasonen confirmed the contested palkka-avoimuus bill will not be ready in time. Government proposal goes to Eduskunta in midsummer week; entry into force shifted to late autumn / year-end 2026. Major shift vs W18: previous 2026-05-18 effective date is no longer valid. Finland will miss 7 Jun 2026 EU deadline.

sources.title: Ministeri Grahn-Laasonen: palkka-avoimuus ei valmistu ajoissa

FI.weeklyCheckSummary: Downgraded GREEN → AMBER: bill pushed to late autumn 2026; Finland will miss the 7 Jun EU deadline.

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.headline: Draft bill at Council of State; effect 1 Jan 2027 confirmed
NL.summary: Minister of Social Affairs submitted transposition draft to Raad van State (Jan 2026). NL targets 1 Jan 2027 effective date despite Commission rejecting postponement on 18 Dec 2025; Tweede Kamer vote not yet scheduled. Commission warned slip risks infringement.
NL.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: 1 Jan 2027 effective date holds; Commission infringement risk remains.

DK.name: Denmark
DK.headline: Bill in consultation; election uncertainty; effect 1 Jan 2027
DK.summary: Beskæftigelsesministeriet draft (26 Feb 2026) hearing closed 27 Mar. Government still proposes 1 Jan 2027 entry into force; recent parliamentary election call introduces timeline risk (DI / PwC DK).

sources.title: Lovforslag om løngennemsigtighed sendt i høring

DK.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: election uncertainty introduces timeline risk; 1 Jan 2027 still the working target.

CZ.name: Czech Republic
CZ.headline: MPSV draft 26 Mar 2026 in consultation; effective 1 Jan 2028
CZ.summary: MPSV released draft on 26 Mar 2026 in public consultation; minimum-transposition approach. Most provisions take effect 1 Jan 2028; Czechia will miss 7 Jun 2026 deadline by ~18 months.

sources.title: Czechia charts its own course on transposition

CZ.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: W18 baseline date corrected to 26 Mar; substantive obligations from 1 Jan 2028.

PL.name: Poland
PL.headline: Phase-1 in force since 24 Dec 2025; full transposition draft targets 7 Jun 2026
PL.summary: Recruitment-stage rules (Labour Code amendment) effective 24 Dec 2025. Draft transposing Articles 7–12 published; targeted to enter into force on 7 Jun 2026 alongside EU deadline. Phase-1 already binding; Phase-2 still draft.
PL.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: Phase-1 binding since 24 Dec 2025; Phase-2 draft targeting 7 Jun deadline.

RO.name: Romania
RO.headline: Revised draft consultation closed 8 Apr; awaiting Parliament submission
RO.summary: Ministry of Labour published revised draft 30 Mar 2026 with public consultation to 8 Apr. Text now to be tabled in Parliament; no cabinet adoption observed by 10 May. Tighter Romanian-specific deadlines (30+30 working days for clarifications).

sources.title: Romania revises transposing legislation — key changes
Romania publishes first draft law for consultation
Draft law to transpose EUPTD in Romania

RO.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: text awaits Parliament submission; tighter RO-specific deadlines confirmed.

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.headline: Government approved draft 18 Mar 2026; Seimas debate ongoing, on track for 7 Jun
LT.summary: Lithuanian Government approved Labour Code amendment on 18 Mar 2026; bill before Seimas. Most provisions to enter force 30 Apr / 1 May 2026 (Article 140); pre-employment rights from 7 Jun 2026. Sodra monthly pay reporting is a notable LT-specific addition.
LT.weeklyCheckSummary: Strengthened within AMBER: government approval on 18 Mar; on track for 7 Jun deadline.

LV.name: Latvia
LV.headline: Draft law published Spring 2026; Saeima review continues
LV.summary: Labklājības ministrija draft transposing Directive 2023/970 progressing through Saeima committee review; framework aligns with Directive (gender-neutral criteria, 100+ reporting threshold). No specific Saeima vote date found in May 2026.
LV.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: Saeima committee review continues; no specific vote date scheduled.

CY.name: Cyprus
CY.headline: Draft 'Equal Remuneration Law of 2026' progressing; not yet enacted
CY.summary: After Nov–Dec 2025 consultation and a Jan 2026 update, the bill 'Strengthening Implementation of Equal Remuneration through Wage Transparency Law of 2026' is moving through formal adoption process. Final text expected before 7 Jun 2026 deadline.

sources.title: Cyprus sets its course in new draft bill
Draft law to transpose EUPTD in Cyprus

CY.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: bill in formal adoption process; final text expected before 7 Jun.

BE.name: Belgium
BE.headline: Min. Clarinval reaffirms full transposition by 7 Jun 2026
BE.summary: In Committee on Social Affairs (week of 13 Apr 2026) Clarinval pledged a federal private-sector draft soon. FWB rules already in force; Flemish public-sector draft pending parliamentary vote. Federal text still not circulated publicly; regional asymmetry persists.

keyDates.label: Decree in force (public sector, French Community)
Minister Clarinval confirms full federal transposition

BE.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: Clarinval pledge re-confirmed; federal text still not public.

IE.name: Ireland
IE.headline: Heads of Bill not drafted; phased transposition, no penalties for missed deadline
IE.summary: Department of Children, Disability and Equality confirmed phased approach; pre-employment obligations to be prioritised. Spring 2026 Legislative Programme drops the bill from priority drafting list. Pay Gap reporting law (active since 2022) remains in force; full Directive transposition will slip past 7 Jun 2026.

sources.title: Ireland: Update on EUPTD transposition and what employers can expect
Employers won't be 'penalised' as Ireland to miss deadline

IE.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: phased rollout confirmed; full Directive transposition will slip past 7 Jun.

ES.name: Spain
ES.headline: Public consultation on Real Decreto closed 8 May 2026; final text pending
ES.summary: Pre-legislative consultation on the Royal Decree transposing Directive 2023/970 closed 8 May 2026. Ministry of Labour now drafting final text to meet 7 Jun 2026 deadline; no BOE publication yet.

sources.title: Trabajo lanza a contrarreloj la transposición

ES.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: consultation closed 8 May; final text drafting against 7 Jun deadline.

SI.name: Slovenia
SI.headline: Nov-2025 consultation draft; no published bill as of late Apr 2026
SI.summary: Draft published 7 Nov 2025 for inter-ministerial consultation. Independent trackers as of 30 Apr 2026 still report no published parliamentary bill; deadline at risk. Some tier-2 trackers describe SI as 'no published draft' — flagging for synthesis review.
SI.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated (low confidence): inter-ministerial consultation only; no parliamentary bill.

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.headline: No published draft; 29 May target slipping
BG.summary: Despite a 29 May 2026 government action-plan target, no draft has been tabled in the Народно събрание and no public consultation text exists as of early May 2026. Realistic adoption window slipping to H2 2026.

sources.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive in Bulgaria

BG.weeklyCheckSummary: Downgraded AMBER → RED: 29 May target slipping; no draft tabled, no first reading scheduled.

DE.name: Germany
DE.headline: BMBFSFJ in internal consultation; no Referentenentwurf published
DE.summary: Lead ministry is BMBFSFJ (not BMAS); commission report (Nov 2025) under review. Q1-2026 draft target missed; no Referentenentwurf publicly released. June-2026 deadline will be missed. Correction vs W18: lead is BMBFSFJ under Min. Karin Prien, not BMAS.

sources.title: Implementation of the Pay Transparency Directive — Commission recommendations

DE.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: lead ministry corrected to BMBFSFJ (not BMAS); Q1 draft target missed; deadline will slip.

FR.name: France
FR.headline: Preliminary draft to social partners 6 Mar 2026; bill not yet tabled
FR.summary: Preliminary transposition draft sent to social partners on 6 Mar 2026; consultation launched 19 Mar. Parliamentary debate now expected end-2026; France will miss 7 Jun 2026 deadline. Most provisions effective ~1 year post-promulgation.

sources.title: France and EU Commission move forward on Pay Directive
France Pay Transparency Draft Law — Key differences

FR.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: parliamentary debate expected end-2026; deadline will be missed.

AT.name: Austria
AT.headline: No Begutachtungsentwurf as of May 2026; spring-2026 slot missed
AT.summary: Implementation planned via GlBG and ArbVG amendments. As of Feb-2026 commentary, no consultation draft published; no parliamentary registration found by W20. No tier-1 update since W18; tier-2 advisories confirm no draft.
AT.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: no Begutachtungsentwurf; spring-2026 slot missed.

PT.name: Portugal
PT.headline: No tabled draft; ACT acting on directive spirit
PT.summary: No transposition bill has been published or tabled in the Assembleia da República as of 10 May 2026. The Labour Authority (ACT) is already enforcing the directive's spirit; Portugal will miss 7 Jun 2026 deadline. Press confirms 86% of companies unprepared.

sources.title: Empresas pouco mais de um mês para se adaptarem

PT.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: no draft tabled; ACT already enforcing in spirit.

LU.name: Luxembourg
LU.headline: Still no projet de loi filed; Q1 expectation missed
LU.summary: Deloitte LU and BSP confirm no draft transposition bill tabled by early May 2026. Q1 2026 expectation has lapsed; deadline 7 Jun 2026 effectively unachievable. No Chambre des Députés activity in late April / May 2026.

sources.title: Pay Transparency: What will change in Luxembourg

LU.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: no projet de loi filed; deadline effectively unachievable.

HR.name: Croatia
HR.headline: No draft published; Croatia among the laggards
HR.summary: As of late Apr 2026 no draft transposition text published; working group active at ministry level only. Pinsent Masons and L&E Global flag Croatia as one of the laggards. Anti-Discrimination Act expected to be amended; no Sabor scheduling.
HR.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: no Sabor scheduling; Croatia among the laggards.

GR.name: Greece
GR.headline: No draft published; specialist committee still preparing framework
GR.summary: No draft law (Σχέδιο Νόμου) or public consultation released. Specialist committee preparing national framework; report expected by 28 Feb 2026 but no public output confirmed. Greece will likely miss the deadline.

sources.title: Greece's EUPTD draft still in progress

GR.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: specialist committee report not public; Greece will likely miss deadline.

HU.name: Hungary
HU.headline: Specialist committee report due 28 Feb 2026; no public draft
HU.summary: Specialist committee preparing national framework with 28 Feb 2026 report deadline; no draft bill or consultation paper published as of W20. No tier-1 confirmation found.
HU.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated (low confidence): no public draft; tier-1 confirmation pending.

SE.name: Sweden
SE.headline: Government still seeks Directive renegotiation; no bill to Riksdag
SE.summary: Government maintains it will not submit a transposition bill to the Riksdag and is pushing for renegotiation at EU level. Commission reiterated in Dec 2025 that all MS must meet deadline.

sources.title: Sweden seeks renegotiation — Is the tide turning against Brussels?

SE.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: still resisting; no public update in May 2026.

EE.name: Estonia
EE.headline: Government confirms it will accept EU fine rather than transpose on time
EE.summary: Economic Affairs Minister stated Estonia would pay a fine rather than burden businesses; transposition will not meet 7 Jun 2026 deadline. Position confirmed across multiple Baltic legal trackers in May 2026.

sources.title: Eesti ütleb võrdse palga direktiivile ei

EE.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: government will accept EU fine rather than transpose on time.
